in the gap. Plans that use a co-payment amount rather than a coinsurance 
percentage will not have a value in this field. The coverage gap is technically referred to as the initial 
coverage limit (ICL) for the Part D benefit. 

CMS instructed plans to calculate this amount by including the average expected cost-sharing amount 
in dollars that a beneficiary would be expected to pay at a standard/network retail pharmacy for a 
one-month supply of drugs. This estimate should be based on available
